title: Intense Mustardy Chinese Tomatoes Fried Rice
description: A pillowy, mustard-infused fried rice made with vine tomatoes and fermented soy sauce, cooked in a single Dutch oven for a quick, sattvic-friendly monsoon treat.
instructions: Rinse 1 cup jasmine rice under cold water until water runs clear.
In a Dutch oven, bring 1.25 cup water to a boil; add rice, cover, and simmer on low for 15 minutes until tender.
While rice cooks, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a separate Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add 1 tsp mustard seeds to the hot oil and toast until fragrant, about 30 seconds, then stir in 1 tbsp fermented soy sauce.
Add diced vine tomatoes, grated ginger, chili flakes, and a pinch of salt; cook until tomatoes soften, 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Gently fold in 100g cubed tofu and cooked rice, ensuring even coating, and cook for an additional 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Stir in 2 tbsp chopped scallions, taste, and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, garnishing with extra scallions if desired.
